evidence.description: Lines 144-149: invoke_app_startup is a STUB that hardcodes Err(Unconfigured). FIND-203 review explicitly required: 'Wire them to the existing app-startup, configure-vault, and VaultPath::try_new implementations in promptnotes/src-tauri/src/domain/'. The wiring is absent.

try_vault_path uses raw_path.starts_with('/') — Unix-only path validation. Spec EC catalog covers cross-platform considerations (EC-15 OS_PATH_MAX, EC-14 symlinks). Windows absolute paths (C:\foo) would be incorrectly rejected as NotAbsolute.

invoke_configure_vault validates the path is a directory but does not persist settings. After successful configure, no Settings.save invocation occurs, so subsequent invoke_app_startup calls cannot read the saved vault path.

appShellStore object literal exposes _store.set and _store.update as public methods. The PROP-011 audit (effectful-isolation.test.ts) is a static text scan only; the type system does NOT prevent any module from calling appShellStore.set(...). The 'restriction' is detection-after-fact, not prevention.

REQ-001 'Loading set BEFORE invoke' test subscribes to appShellStore but never asserts that 'Loading' was observed during the pending phase — only checks the final 'Configured' value. The Loading-precedes-IPC contract is not actually verified.
